en: It's also a Winslow-Hulman ripoff, except you got Whitey rowing about there.
ko: 그건 윈슬로-훌먼의 표절이기도 해, 대신 화이트가 보트를 젓고 있어.

en: It's an art one. It wasn't very good. That's not really what concerns me, though.
ko: 하지만 그게 내가 걱정하는 건 아니야.

en: What concerns you? Just the coloring. You know what the real bitch of it is?
It's paint by number. Is it color by number?
ko: 숫자에 맞춰 색칠하는 거야? 색깔이 나한텐 매력적이야.

en: Because the colors are fascinating to me. Are they really? What about that?
I think you're about one step away from cutting your fucking ear off. Really?
Oh, yeah. Think I should move to the south of France, change my name to Vincent?
ko: 남프랑스로 이사 가서 이름을 빈센트로 바꿔야 할까?

en: You ever heard the saying, any port in a storm? Yeah.
ko: 폭풍우 속에서 어떤 항구라도 괜찮다는 말 들어본 적 있어? 응.

en: Yeah, maybe that means you. In what way?
ko: 응, 아마 너를 의미하는 걸지도. 어떤 의미로?

en: Maybe you're in the middle of a storm, a big fucking storm. Yeah.
ko: 아마 너는 큰 폭풍 한가운데 있어.

en: The sky's falling on your head. The waves are crashing over your little boat.
ko: 하늘이 너의 머리 위로 무너지고, 파도가 너의 작은 보트를 덮치고,

en: The oars are about to snap. You're just pissing your pants.
You're crying for the harbor. So maybe you did what you gotta do to get out.
ko: 그래서 너는 나가려고 필요한 일을 했을지도 몰라.

en: You know, maybe you became a psychologist. Bingo. That's it.
Let me do my job now. You start with me. Come on.
ko: 이제 내 일을 하게 해줘. 너는 나부터 시작해. 자, 시작해.

en: Maybe you married the wrong woman. Maybe you should watch your mouth.
Watch it right there, chief, all right?
